q: Last year, Ranjan's monthly salary was ₹14,300 and this year his monthly salary is ₹16,640. What is the percentage increase in Ranjan's monthly salary in this year over his monthly salary last year?

In December 2021, which state Government inaugurated the 'Pink Force' of Police to enhance safety, security of women and children?

An object is placed at a distance of 25 cm from a concave mirror, and its image is formed at a distance of 30 cm from the mirror. Find the magnification and the nature of the image formed by this object.

q:
Which of the following is NOT an example of a redox reaction?
(i) ZnO + C → Zn + CO
(ii) MnO₂ + 4HCl → MnCl₂ + 2H₂O + Cl₂
(iii) 4Na + O₂ → 2Na₂O
(iv) AgNO₃ + NaCl → AgCl + NaNO₃

If the given equation is balanced by interchanging the signs and numbers indicated, then which of the following options should replace the question mark (?), and which two numbers should be interchanged?
# 9 1 @ 4 ÷ 7 * 5 3 @ 1 * 5 4 @ 3 5 # 2 − 6 1 # 2 6 9 < 3 8 # 2 % 2 @ 7
Division sign should come in place of question mark, and the equation should hold true.
drop: Symbol-substitution puzzle with garbled symbol string; tick reliable

q: Article 279A is related to which constitutional body?

Three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements.
Statements:
All phones are tablets.
No tablet is a calculator.
All drives are calculators.
Conclusions:
I. Some drives are phones.
II. Some calculators are phones.
III. No tablet is a drive.
o: Only conclusions I and II follow
Only conclusions I and III follow

q:
If '+' means '−', '−' means '×', '×' means '+', '÷' means '−', what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
16 + 4 − 8 ÷ 3 × 2 = ?
The distance between the pole and the centre of curvature of a spherical mirror, in terms of its focal length f, is equal to ______.

The armature of an electric motor consists of which of the following part/parts?
(i) Soft iron core
(ii) Coil
(iii) Magnets
Which of the following 'state – major language' pairs has been INCORRECTLY matched?

To reach point B from point A, Anita should walk 70 m towards the west, then she should turn right and walk 70 m, then she should turn left and walk 150 m, then she should turn left and walk 70 m, then she should turn left and walk 90 m, then she should turn right and walk 100 m, and finally she should turn left and walk 130 m. How far and in which direction is point B from point A? (All turns are 90 degree turns only.)

The following graph and table show the performance of three persons – A, B and C – in three tests (Test 1, Test 2 and Test 3). If the maximum marks for Test 1 and Test 2 are both 80, and for Test 3 are 100, then considering the performance in all three tests, by what percentage are the marks obtained by A more than those obtained by C?
drop: Requires a line graph and table of marks that cannot be linearized into text

Based on the given statement, two conclusions are drawn. Find out which conclusion is true based on the statement.
Statement: Z > R ≥ V > P = Q ≤ N < S
Conclusions:
I. P > S
II. Z = Q

o: Potato juice contains proteins, which turn blue black with iodine
Potato juice contains fats, which turn blue black with iodine
Potato juice contains starch, which turns blue black with iodine

q: If 15 March 2022 was a Tuesday, what day of the week was 15 March 2020?
Mawsynram is the wettest place on earth and it is situated in ______.

S, T, U, V, W and X live on six different floors of the same building. The lowermost floor in the building is numbered 1, the floor above it is numbered 2, and so on till the topmost floor is numbered 6.
V lives on the lowermost floor. X and U each live on an even numbered floor. T lives exactly between U and W. W lives on floor number 2. Who lives on floor number 5?

Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets.
(12, 18, 18)
(45, 35, 25)
(NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/deleting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
Find the ratio of (sin² 22° + sin² 68°) and (cos² 33° + cos² 57°).

Answer the question asked on the basis of the following number series.
5 1 2 6 3 8 6 5 3 6 5 3 2 5 6 9 5 1 7 2
How many such numbers are there in the above series, each of which is immediately preceded by an even number and also immediately followed by an even number?
Which action occurring in the human digestive system is similar to the emulsifying action of soap on dirt?
o: The breaking down of large fat globules into smaller globules by bile salts
Providing an alkaline medium by bile juice
Assisting in the digestion of starch by the enzymes of bile juice
Providing an acidic medium by bile juice
